name: Pedals dels Ports: Rossell and Vallibona Loop
name_original: Vallibona - Bel — Circular desde Rosell por el Río Cervol
description: Experience the demanding Pedals dels Ports: Rossell and Vallibona Loop, a 32.8-mile mountain bike route through rugged terrain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Rossell features challenging routes through varied terrain, characterized by significant elevation changes and natural landscapes. The region's mountain bike trails often traverse forested areas and lead to viewpoints over reservoirs. Riders can expect demanding climbs and descents across these routes. Best mountain bike trails around Rossell The most popular mountain bike trail is Pedals dels Ports: Rossell and Vallibona Loop, a 32.8 miles (52.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through the Ports region. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ulldecona Reservoir – View of Fredes loop from la Sénia, a difficult 38.8 miles (62.5 km) path. This route offers extensive riding with views over the Ulldecona Reservoir and towards Fredes. Local mountain bikers also love the Faig Pare (Father Beech) – Ulldecona Reservoir loop from la Sénia, a 35.3 miles (56.9 km) trail leading through forested areas and past the Ulldecona Reservoir, often completed in about 5 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Rossell is defined by challenging climbs, forested sections, and routes around reservoirs. The network offers options for various ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails. The routes in Rossell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Rossell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Rossell?
A: Rossell offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 50 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: What are the typical terrain and landscapes like for mountain biking in Rossell?
A: Mountain biking in Rossell is characterized by varied terrain, including challenging routes through forested areas and significant elevation changes. Many trails offer views over reservoirs and traverse the rugged Ports region.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Rossell?
A: Yes, Rossell provides options for various ability levels. While many routes are difficult, there are 7 easy trails and 27 moderate trails available, perfect for those new to mountain biking or looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some scenic natural features or landmarks I can see along the mountain bike trails in Rossell?
A: Many routes in Rossell offer stunning natural sights. You can ride past the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2801736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portell de l'Infern gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features ancient trees like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3951882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Millennial Olive Tree of the Four Legs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Rossell?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Rossell are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932024423/pedals-dels-ports-rossell-and-vallibona-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedals dels Ports: Rossell and Vallibona Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir – View of Fredes loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular routes.

Q: What is the typical duration and distance of mountain bike trails in Rossell?
A: Trail lengths and durations vary significantly. Difficult routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932024423/pedals-dels-ports-rossell-and-vallibona-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedals dels Ports: Rossell and Vallibona Loop&lt;/a&gt; can be around 32.8 miles (52.7 km) and take over 5 hours, while others, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14889313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faig Pare (Father Beech) – Ulldecona Reservoir loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt;, are about 35.3 miles (56.9 km) and also take around 5 hours.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Rossell?
A: The mountain bike trails in Rossell are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders frequently praise the challenging climbs, the scenic forested sections, and the routes that offer views around the reservoirs.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by waterfalls in Rossell?
A: Yes, you can find trails that lead to natural attractions like waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight in the area, and some routes will take you near it.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Rossell?
A: Mountain biking in Rossell is known for significant elevation changes. Many difficult routes feature substantial climbs, with some trails gaining over 1,000 meters (3,280 feet) in elevation, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932024423/pedals-dels-ports-rossell-and-vallibona-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedals dels Ports: Rossell and Vallibona Loop&lt;/a&gt; which has nearly 1,400 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of reservoirs in Rossell?
A: Absolutely. Many mountain bike routes in Rossell are designed to showcase the region's reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir – View of Fredes loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering extensive riding with panoramic views over the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the mountain bike trails?
A: While the focus is on natural landscapes, some routes may pass near historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5116474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Saints Abdón and Senén (Fredes)&lt;/a&gt; and the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5527451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallibona&lt;/a&gt; are points of interest in the wider region that some trails might approach or pass through.
tips: Vallibona is a charming destination to visit! This small town, located in the Els Ports region of Castellón, has a medieval charm that will transport you back in time.
Small, but very picturesque town in the interior of Castellón, with some services and bars. Take the opportunity to walk through its beautiful streets, you won't waste much time doing it.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Pedals dels Ports: Rossell and Vallibona Loop?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike loop is approximately 52.7 km long with over 1390 meters of elevation gain. While the estimated moving time is around 5 hours, plan for a full day to account for technical sections, breaks, and enjoying the scenery. It's considered a difficult route, so pace yourself accordingly.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses a complex and rugged massif, offering diverse and disparate terrain. You'll encounter a mix of forest tracks, trails, and mountain paths, including technical sections, 'sendas' (singletracks), and 'trialeras' (technical downhill sections). Expect significant climbs and descents through deep ravines and past steep rock formations. It's not for those who prefer flat or pristine tracks.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as 'difficult' and is considered demanding and laborious. It features significant elevation changes and technical sections that require a minimum level of physical fitness and mountain biking experience. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Rossell. You can typically find parking within the village to begin your ride. As it's a loop, you'll return to your starting point.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers spectacular ravines, geological formations, and an amazing variety of landscapes, including beech forests and Mediterranean beauty. You'll pass through charming, historic villages like Vallibona and Bel Village, and enjoy views of the valley and the Barranco de la Gatellera. Keep an eye out for wild goats!

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The region experiences Mediterranean weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder conditions or even snow at higher elevations, making the already challenging terrain more difficult. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Pedals dels Ports: Rossell and Vallibona Loop?
faqs.answer: This specific loop is part of the larger Pedals dels Ports route, which traverses the Parc Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride the trails themselves. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning nature conservation within the Natural Park.

faqs.question: Is the Pedals dels Ports: Rossell and Vallibona Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the region, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially within the Parc Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà, to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on technical sections. Given the route's difficulty and length, consider if it's suitable for your dog's fitness level.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other trails. Notably, about 29% of the loop follows the official Pedals dels Ports trail. You'll also ride parts of the Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós, Camí de Conquesta, and various local paths like Camí de Castell de Cabres and Camí de Bel a la Pobla.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a mountain bike ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and remote nature, essential items include sufficient water (there are limited resupply points), high-energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes/patches), a first-aid kit, navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app), appropriate clothing layers, and sun protection. A helmet is mandatory.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the route for refreshments?
faqs.answer: You'll pass through villages like Vallibona and Bel Village, which may offer limited options for refreshments or a meal, especially during peak season. However, services can be scarce in this wild region, so it's best to be self-sufficient with food and water.
